opinion.h: CAROLINA AMUSEMENT CO. v. MARTIN, 367 U.S. 904 (1961)
CAROLINA AMUSEMENT CO. ET AL. v. MARTIN, SHERIFF, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF SOUTH CAROLINA. No. 424. Decided June 19, 1961.
opinion.p: J. D. Todd, Jr. and Chester D. Ward, Jr. for appellants.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS and MR. JUSTICE BRENNAN are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
367 U.S. 904 BIRDWELL ET AL. v. KIRKLAND ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF CIVIL APPEALS OF TEXAS, SECOND SUPREME JUDICIAL DISTRICT. No. 914. Decided June 19, 1961.
Sidney E. Dawson and Townes Loring Dawson for appellants.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
